seoTitle: How to Find and Use a QR Code on Slack
metaDescription: Locate your personal Slack QR code for fast mobile sign-in, or generate a channel invite QR code to bring in external collaborators.
intro: Slack's QR codes aren't just for signing into mobile, they can also help you invite people to channels or share your contact details quickly. This guide covers finding your personal QR code and generating a channel invitation code.

steps.heading: Open your profile identity panel
steps.body: Click your profile avatar and launch your account identity settings.

steps.heading: View your personal QR code
steps.body: Click your profile picture dropdown and select 'View Personal QR Code' to display your digital badge.

steps.heading: Save or share the code
steps.body: Click 'Save to Device' to download the image, or share it directly with a contact for quick sign-in.

steps.heading: Scan a code from your phone
steps.body: Open the Slack mobile app, tap the search bar scanner icon, and align your camera with the code you want to scan.

steps.heading: Generate a channel invite QR code
steps.body: Open a public project channel, click Channel Details, and generate a location-based invitation code for external partners.

faq.question: Can I regenerate my QR code if it's been shared too widely?
faq.answer: Yes, you can revoke and regenerate your personal QR code from the same identity panel for security.

faq.question: Do channel invite QR codes expire?
faq.answer: Some channel invite codes may have expiration settings depending on how your workspace admin configured sharing.

faq.question: Is scanning a QR code safe for signing into a new device?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as you're scanning a code generated by your own trusted account rather than one shared by someone else.

faq.question: Can I print my QR code for networking events?
faq.answer: Yes, since it's a downloadable image, you can print it or display it digitally when sharing your contact details.
